name: Standon
overview: Standon is a tiny hamlet in the civil parish of Hursley in the City of Winchester district of Hampshire, lying about half a mile north of Hursley village where the A3090 curves towards Winchester, roughly midway between Winchester and Romsey. It is a small group of houses and farms set in rolling countryside; the Victoria County History describes it as a cluster of cottages and farmsteads north of Hursley. It has no facilities of its own, with the village church, primary school and pubs in Hursley, and full amenities in Winchester (~4-5 miles).
character: A very small, quiet rural hamlet of houses and farmsteads on rising ground between Hursley and Winchester. The feel is secluded and agricultural, with countryside walks nearby and the well-kept village of Hursley (known for its tall chimneys) close at hand.
amenities: No shop or pub in Standon itself
Village shop area, All Saints Church, primary school and two pubs (King's Head, The Dolphin) in Hursley (~0.5 mi)
IBM Hursley Park nearby (major local employer)
Full shopping, supermarkets and services in Winchester (~4-5 mi)
Chandler's Ford retail and rail (~4 mi)

schools.name: John Keble CofE Primary School, Hursley

commutes.notes: Via the A3090; mainline station to London Waterloo
Via the A3090 the other direction
Via Chandler's Ford and the M3/M27
From Winchester station (SWR), drive ~12 min to the station

prices.summary: Hursley and Standon are a sought-after location between Winchester and Romsey; housing is dominated by detached and period country houses, commonly £600k-£1.5m+ with larger properties well above. Stock is very limited and sells quickly.

thingsToDo: Walking around Merdon Castle earthworks and Hursley Park estate
Countryside footpaths between Hursley and Farley Mount Country Park
Hursley village (Church of All Saints, historic cottages)
Easy access to Winchester's culture, cathedral and shops
placesToEat: Wide choice of restaurants in Winchester (~4-5 mi)
pros: Quiet, secluded setting midway between Winchester and Romsey
Good village primary (John Keble) and two pubs in Hursley nearby
Quick drive to Winchester's mainline and amenities
Attractive countryside and country park within reach
cons: Very small hamlet with no amenities of its own; car essential
Expensive and very limited housing stock
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a small, secluded country hamlet with good schooling in Hursley and a short commute to Winchester, with a budget for the premium local market.

sources.label: Ofsted - John Keble CofE Primary School
